questions.original_question: 路特斯是否仍在销售燃油车
questions.answer: 是的，路特斯在2024年仍然销售燃油车。尽管路特斯科技主要专注于设计和开发豪华电池电动汽车（BEV），但通过其全资子公司和可变利益实体（VIE）及其子公司，路特斯在全球范围内继续销售豪华燃油跑车。例如，Emira等车型在市场上仍然保持较高的售价和良好的销售表现。以上信息基于2025年1月15日之前的知识。

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 27. REVENUES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated substantial revenue growth across its various service lines, as detailed in the "Revenue Breakdown by Service Line" table for the years ending December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022. The company's revenues are segmented into sales to third parties and related parties, providing insights into the performance of each area. In 2024, Lotus reported total revenues of $924.3 million, a significant increase from $679.0 million in 2023 and just $9.6 million in 2022.

The company's standout performance was in the sales of battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models, which generated $627.7 million in 2024, up from $464.2 million in 2023, with no revenue recorded in 2022. Sales of sports cars also rose to $211.8 million in 2024, compared to $183.2 million the previous year. Total sales of goods to third parties reached $872.9 million in 2024, while related party sales contributed an additional $18.2 million, bringing the subtotal for total goods sold to $891.1 million.

Service revenues presented a mixed picture, with R&D services to third parties declining to $1.2 million, while other services increased to $7.7 million. Overall, total service revenue from third parties was $8.9 million, and related party services generated $24.4 million, culminating in total service revenue of $33.3 million in 2024. The "Others" category includes a variety of services such as training programs, installation of intelligent driving systems, maintenance services, and vehicle internet connection services, while "2 Others" primarily consists of commission service fees. This comprehensive breakdown underscores Lotus Technology Inc.'s robust performance and growth trajectory across its service lines.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated substantial revenue growth across its various service lines, as detailed in the "Revenue Breakdown by Service Line" table for the years ending December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022. Overall, total service revenue from third parties was $8.9 million, and related party services generated $24.4 million, culminating in total service revenue of $33.3 million in 2024. The "Others" category includes a variety of services such as training programs, installation of intelligent driving systems, maintenance services, and vehicle internet connection services, while "2 Others" primarily consists of commission service fees. This comprehensive breakdown underscores Lotus Technology Inc.'s robust performance and growth trajectory across its service lines.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology’s American Depositary Shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq on February 23, 2024, under the ticker symbol “LOT.” New York and Singapore - February 20, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, announced today that their previously proposed business combination is expected to be completed on February 22, 2024. The combined company will retain Lotus Technology Inc.'s name as “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American Depositary Shares (ADS) will commence trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “LOT” on February 23, 2024. The business combination was approved by L CAA shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting held on February 2, 2024. Integrating Lotus Technology’s sports car DNA with a new generation of premium lifestyle vehicles, Lotus Technology Inc. is committed to transforming the brand into an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider before its 80th anniversary in 2028. Lotus Technology Inc. has already set this ambitious plan in motion by launching a leading product portfolio of high-performing battery electric vehicles (“BEVs”) ahead of other global luxury automakers. These BEVs include Eletre, a new breed of all-electric hyper-SUV powered by Lotus Technology’s proprietary 800-volt EPA, and Emeya, an all-electric hyper-GT among the most advanced vehicles of its kind in the world.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is poised to make its debut on the Nasdaq stock exchange on February 23, 2024, trading under the ticker symbol "LOT." This significant milestone follows the completion of its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), which is set to finalize on February 22, 2024, after receiving shareholder approval on February 2, 2024. Lotus Tech aims to transform into a fully electric and sustainable luxury mobility provider by 2028, coinciding with its 80th anniversary, and plans to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027. The company has already introduced high-performing battery electric vehicles, such as the Eletre hyper-SUV and the Emeya hyper-GT, with plans for two additional models in the next two years. Since announcing the merger, Lotus Tech has secured over $880 million in financing commitments, marking one of the largest de-SPAC transactions since 2023. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed enthusiasm for the company's growth as a public entity, while L CAA Co-CEO Chinta Bhagat highlighted their shared vision for establishing a leading luxury electric vehicle brand. To commemorate its public listing, Lotus Tech will ring the Nasdaq opening bell on February 23, an event that will be live-streamed for viewers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York and Singapore - January 9, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, announced today that its intelligent driving arm, Lotus Robotics, will showcase numerous proprietary next-generation autonomous driving software and hardware solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, USA. The technologies on display, along with Lotus Technology Inc.’s recent receipt of approval to test conditional automated driving (“Level 3” or “L3”) functions on highways in part of China, demonstrate Lotus Technology Inc.’s progress in executing the brand’s Vision80 business transformation strategy and further advancing its global research and development prowess. The ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy software being showcased at CES provide solutions for advanced driver assistance systems, remotely assisted operation, and cloud data management respectively. The technologies have been employed in Lotus Technology Inc. vehicles, contributing to their autonomous driving capabilities. The hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, for example, both feature cutting-edge autonomous driving hardware, aviation-level security and redundancy including dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips, as well as Lotus Technology Inc.’s industry-leading cognition module, which can intelligently identify and avoid risks on the road. Lotus Robotics is also displaying hardware that it is developing as part of efforts to accelerate the adoption of self-driving technology, including the ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

title: Lotus Technology Showcases
Autonomous Driving Progress at CES 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is set to showcase its advanced autonomous driving solutions at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) in Las Vegas, highlighting its commitment to innovation and safety. The company's intelligent driving division, Lotus Robotics, will present proprietary software and hardware, including the ROBO Soul, ROBO Matrix, and ROBO Galaxy, which enhance driver assistance, enable remote operations, and manage cloud data. These technologies are integral to Lotus Tech's vehicles, such as the hyper SUV Eletre and hyper GT Emeya, which feature cutting-edge hardware like dual NVIDIA DRIVE Orin chips and a unique cognition module for risk management.

Recently, Lotus Tech received approval to test Level 3 automated driving on highways in Wuxi, China, a pilot zone for vehicle connectivity. This allows Eletre drivers to utilize its L3 capabilities on designated highways, supported by the vehicle's advanced Level 4-ready hardware, including a deployable LIDAR system and multiple sensors for enhanced perception. Lotus Robotics is also promoting self-driving technology through its ROBOCube intelligent street cleaner.

As part of its Vision80 business transformation strategy, Lotus Tech is enhancing its global R&D capabilities, with CEO Mr. Qingfeng Feng expressing optimism about the future of intelligent driving. The company is also nearing the completion of a merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, expected to finalize in early 2024, which will transition Lotus Tech to a public entity listed on Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." Visitors can explore Lotus Robotics' innovations at booth 9217 in the Tech East AI pavilion from January 9-12.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. (Lotus Tech), a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, marked a significant milestone by going public on February 23, 2024, with its shares trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." title: 2024-AYEAROFGROWTH 
summary: Lotus Cars is undergoing a strategic transformation to become an intelligent luxury mobility provider, emphasizing the development and integration of Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) models. This shift aims to enhance sales volume and profit margins while positioning the company as a leader in the luxury automotive market. By focusing on advanced technologies and sustainable practices, Lotus is set to attract a growing customer base interested in eco-friendly luxury options, which is expected to drive significant growth and profitability.

In 2024, the company plans to triple its delivery volume and strengthen client relationships by introducing four new delivery models. Key initiatives will include optimizing operational efficiency, reducing costs, and implementing best practices to streamline operations. Additionally, targeted marketing, improved customer service, and a refined product assortment will be prioritized to maximize revenue per location, with continuous assessment and adaptation being crucial to achieving these goals.

Performance metrics from 2018 to 2024 indicate a remarkable growth trajectory for Lotus Cars. Sales volume increased from an average of 1,200 units annually (2018-2022) to 6,970 units in 2023, with projections for 2024 reaching 26,000 units. Revenue followed a similar trend, jumping from an average of $100 million during 2018-2022 to $679 million in 2023, with estimates for 2024 ranging between $2,500 million and $2,700 million. The gross profit margin also improved significantly, rising from an average of 3% (2018-2022) to 15% in 2023, with projections for 2024 suggesting further growth to between 17% and 19%. 

Overall, these metrics highlight Lotus Cars' ambitious trajectory towards becoming a leader in the luxury electric vehicle market, with a strong focus on growth in production, revenue, and profitability.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In addition to state-level credits, the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) require all original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) to meet minimum greenhouse gas (GHG) emission and Corporate Average Fuel Economy (CAFE) standards applicable to light-duty vehicles. Lotus Technology will also benefit from these regulations as a developer of zero-emission vehicles because each electric vehicle will generate GHG and CAFE credits which can be sold to other manufacturers. These standards require fleetwide increases in fuel economy and decreases in GHG emissions from internal combustion engine equipped vehicles produced by all manufacturers. Both the EPA and NHTSA are expected to announce additional standards for at least model years 2027 – 2030 in the spring of 2023 that will require further fleetwide increases in fuel economy and decreases in GHG emissions. Moreover, the Biden Administration also announced a goal of 50% electric vehicle (EV) sales by 2030. With the more stringent CAFE and GHG emission standards expected, Lotus Technology may be positioned to monetize the credits earned for selling zero-emission vehicles in the US.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Regulations on Sustainability and Environmental Regulations 
summary: The electric vehicle industry operates within a highly regulated framework focused on sustainability and environmental practices, with increasing regulations anticipated in the future. Compliance with laws governing water usage, air emissions, recycling, energy sources, and hazardous materials management is essential at various levels—international, national, and local. As an electric vehicle developer, we are committed to adhering to these regulations, particularly as many countries, including those in the European Union (EU) and the United Kingdom (UK), implement mandates for zero-emission vehicles by deadlines as early as 2035.

In the EU, vehicle manufacturers must comply with regulations such as EU Regulation 715/2007, which establishes testing procedures for energy consumption and emissions, and Directive 2005/64/EC, which emphasizes recyclability and sustainability. The EU's Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive, effective from January 2023, expands non-financial reporting requirements for large companies, focusing on environmental and social governance. Similarly, the UK has retained EU regulations post-Brexit and is developing its own frameworks for CO2 emissions and zero-emission vehicle mandates.

In the United States, manufacturers must navigate a complex regulatory landscape that includes compliance with federal laws set by the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA). Our all-electric vehicles generate compliance credits that can be sold to other manufacturers, benefiting from regulations that require an increase in the production of zero-emission vehicles. The Biden Administration aims for 50% of vehicle sales to be electric by 2030, indicating a favorable environment for monetizing these credits.

Overall, as we expand our product portfolio, we remain vigilant in monitoring regulatory developments across all regions, ensuring compliance while leveraging opportunities to generate and sell emission credits in response to stringent environmental standards.

If the more stringent corporate average fuel economy and greenhouse gas emission standards are finalized, Lotus Technology may be positioned to monetize the credits it may earn for selling zero-emission vehicles in the U.S.

title: US 
summary: The company plans to sell its vehicles in the U.S. through state-registered dealers, ensuring compliance with federal, state, and local regulations. Adherence to government permits, licenses, and certifications is mandatory, with potential penalties for violations, including fines and operational cessation. Vehicles must meet standards established by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) and the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), as well as emissions standards set by the California Air Resource Board.

Under the Clean Air Act, the company is required to obtain a Certificate of Conformity from the EPA and a California Executive Order annually for each vehicle model. This regulatory framework is designed to ensure compliance with emissions standards for pollutants and greenhouse gases, alongside consumer labeling requirements.

The company anticipates generating regulatory compliance credits from its all-electric vehicles, which can be sold to other Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs). California's Low-Emission Vehicle Program mandates an increasing production of battery electric vehicles (BEVs), targeting 22% production by 2025 and 100% zero-emission vehicle sales by 2035.

Additionally, federal regulations require OEMs to meet minimum greenhouse gas emissions and corporate average fuel economy standards. The EPA has finalized revised standards for model years 2023-2026, while the NHTSA has set standards for 2024-2026. Proposed regulations for model years 2027-2032 suggest that BEVs should constitute 60% of light-duty vehicle sales by 2030 and 67% by 2032, with an average fleetwide fuel economy target of 58 miles per gallon by 2032. These proposed regulations are still subject to finalization, and if enacted, could enhance the company’s potential to monetize credits from its zero-emission vehicle sales. 

In summary, the company's operations in the U.S. are heavily influenced by a complex regulatory environment that emphasizes emissions compliance, the promotion of electric vehicles, and the potential for generating credits through adherence to these regulations.

summary: The U.S. National Traffic and Motor Vehicle Safety Act requires vehicle manufacturers to certify compliance with Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards (FMVSS), bumper standards, and anti-theft regulations before selling or importing vehicles in the U.S. These standards, enforced by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA), cover various safety and performance criteria aimed at enhancing vehicle safety and reducing accidents. Key areas include Crash Avoidance Standards, which set minimum performance criteria for critical vehicle components, and Crash Worthiness and Occupant Protection Requirements, which focus on safeguarding occupants during collisions through measures like airbags and seatbelts.

Electric vehicles (EVs) face additional safety standards, particularly concerning occupant protection during crashes, battery retention, and preventing electric shock. Compliance with these standards is essential to avoid penalties, and manufacturers must also adhere to corporate average fuel economy standards and consumer information labeling requirements. The Automobile Information and Disclosure Act mandates transparency in vehicle pricing and fuel economy ratings, while the American Automobile Labeling Act requires disclosure of component origin and assembly location.

States can impose additional safety regulations and have the authority to regulate vehicle operations, including licensing and registration. Although there is no comprehensive federal law on personal data management, various federal and state laws govern specific data practices, with states like California leading in stringent privacy regulations.

Currently, there are no mandatory federal standards for Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) or automated driving systems (ADS), but the NHTSA may introduce guidelines in the future. Manufacturers must comply with all regulatory requirements across jurisdictions, including obtaining necessary permits and certifications, which can be complex and costly.

Vehicles must also meet emissions standards set by the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and the California Air Resources Board (CARB). The Clean Air Act requires a Certificate of Conformity for each model year, ensuring compliance with emissions standards. The company anticipates generating regulatory compliance credits from its all-electric vehicles, which can be sold to other manufacturers under California's Low-Emission Vehicle Program.

The EPA and NHTSA enforce federal standards for greenhouse gas emissions and corporate average fuel economy, with recent proposals aiming for significant increases in battery electric vehicle sales and fuel economy by 2032. summary: The demand for automobile sales, particularly luxury vehicles and electric vehicles (EVs), is heavily influenced by a range of economic, political, and social factors. However, consumer attitudes towards EVs are affected by perceptions of quality and safety, performance limitations like driving range and battery recharge times, and concerns about charging infrastructure and service availability. Environmental awareness and government incentives also play a role in driving interest in EVs.

Overall, the interplay of these factors can lead to fluctuations in demand for luxury vehicles, impacting sales and the financial health of automotive companies. If the market for luxury electric vehicles does not expand as anticipated, it could adversely affect business prospects and operational performance.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) maker that designs, develops, and sells luxury lifestyle vehicles under the iconic British brand “Lotus.” With over seven decades of heritage and proven leadership in racetrack and road cars, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. The first lifestyle production vehicle, Eletre, is a new breed of pure electric SUV powered by Lotus’s proprietary 800-volt EPA. Eletre had a cumulative global orderbook of over 8,600 units as of September 30, 2023. Vehicle deliveries commenced in China and Europe in 2023. Production of Emeya is expected to commence in 2024. In addition to Eletre and Emeya, Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully-electric vehicles over the next two years, including a D-segment SUV in 2024 and a BEV sports car in 2025. Lotus Technology also plans to upgrade its models on an ongoing basis.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In December 2022, the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) issued a proposed rulemaking that would authorize original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) to generate marketable compliance credits associated with their battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs) sold in the US under the federal Renewable Fuel Standard (“RFS”) program. Under the proposal, beginning in 2024, OEMs would enter into agreements with producers of renewable electricity derived from biogas or renewable natural gas and would then be authorized to generate and sell compliance credits based on an equivalent quantity of electricity used to charge their BEV or PHEV fleet. Petroleum refiners and importers would be obligated to purchase these credits to meet their annual compliance credit obligations under the RFS program. If finalized, Lotus Technology may also be positioned to potentially monetize these credits based on the electricity charging demand of its zero-emission vehicles sold in the US.
